---
title: "Beginner Course on Microbial Genomics:<br>Applications & Research"
---

A bioinformatics workshop organised by the [Molecular Epidemiology Group](https://www.oucru.org/research/molecular-epidemiology) at Oxford University Clinical Research Unit in May, 2026.

This workshop is a part of the [KLEB-CASAVA](https://www.oucru.org/project/kleb-casava/) project, funded by the UKRI.

Aiming to enhance the local capacity in genomic epidemiology and surveillance, the workshop provides essential bioinformatics skills for Vietnamese clinicians and researchers, whose work focuses on bacterial infectious diseases, but have little to no experience in analysing whole-genome sequencing data. 

# Programme

::: panel-tabset
## Day 0
### Pack your backpack
*Setting up your computational environment and getting comfortable with the Linux environment and command lines.* <br>
[Slides]() | [Practical Guides](guides.qmd) | [Data]()

**Module 0.1: [Setup your workspace](day-0/instructions/setup-environment.qmd)**

**Module 0.2: [Commands & Environments](day-0/instructions/linux-basics.qmd)**

## Day 1
### Meet the genomes
*Introduction to whole-genome sequencing and genome assembly.* <br>
[Slides]() | [Practical Guides](day-1/instructions/index.qmd) | [Data]()

**Module 1.1: Whole-genome sequencing - What? & Why?** 

**Module 1.2: A complete genome - How it look like?** 

**Module 1.3: From raw sequencing data to assemblies** 

## Day 2
### Inspect the genomes
*Assembly and gene-level analysis. Long-read sequencing.* <br>
[Slides]() | [Practical Guides](day-2/instructions/index.qmd) | [Data]()

**Module 2.1: Analyse the assemblies**

**Module 2.2: Long-read sequencing & Hybrid assemblies**

**Module 2.3: Locate & compare genes** 

## Day 3
### Genomic variations and relationships
*Core SNP and phylogenetic analysis.* <br>
[Slides]() | [Practical Guides](day-3/instructions/index.qmd) | [Data]()

**Module 3.1: Read mapping**

**Module 3.2: Phylogenetics**

## Day 4
### Next-generation Metagenomic sequencing (mNGS)
*Read-based metagenomic analysis for AMR surveillance and actionable clinical diagnosis.* <br>
[Slides]() | [Practical Guides](day-4/instructions/index.qmd) | [Data](https://github.com/molepi-oucru/MicroGen-2026/tree/main/day-4/materials)

**Module: mNGS in clinical microbiology**

- **4.1: Workflow setup**
- **4.2: Taxonomy analysis**
- **4.3: AMR analysis and summary**

**Module: mNGS in pathogen detection**

- **4.4: Clinical case**
- **4.5: Signal analysis**
:::

# Instructors

::: {layout-ncol=3}

::: {.organiser-card}
![](website-assets/images/DuyPT.png){.profile-pic}

**Dr. Pham Thanh Duy** <br>
*Head of Molecular Epidemiology Group* <br>
Center for Tropical Medicine and Global Health, Nuffield Department of Clinical Medicine, Oxford University

::: {.organiser-links}
<a href="https://github.com/username" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://linkedin.com/in/username" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.organiser-card}
![](website-assets/images/HaoCT.png){.profile-pic}

**Dr. Chung The Hao** <br>
*Senior Postdoc Fellow* <br>
Saw Swee Hock School of Public Health, National University of Singapore

::: {.organiser-links}

<a href="https://github.com/username" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://linkedin.com/in/username" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.organiser-card}
![](website-assets/images/NguyenMTS.png){.profile-pic}

**Mai Thu Si Nguyen** <br>
*Research Assistant* <br>
Oxford University Clinical Research Unit

::: {.organiser-links}

<a href="https://github.com/username" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://linkedin.com/in/username" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.organiser-card}
![](website-assets/images/QuynhNPN.png){.profile-pic}

**Nguyen Pham Nhu Quynh** <br>
*PhD Student* <br>
Center for Tropical Medicine and Global Health, Nuffield Department of Clinical Medicine, Oxford University

::: {.organiser-links}

<a href="https://github.com/username" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://linkedin.com/in/username" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.organiser-card}
![](website-assets/images/QuanTNM.png){.profile-pic}

**Ton Ngoc Minh Quan** <br>
*Research Assistant* <br>
Oxford University Clinical Research Unit

::: {.organiser-links}

<a href="https://github.com/tnmquann" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="fa-brands fa-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/tnmquann/" aria-label="LinkedIn"><i class="fa-brands fa-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0000" aria-label="ORCID"><i class="fa-brands fa-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

:::


# Acknowledgement

::: {.logo-flex-container}

::: {.funder-card}
![](website-assets/images/UKRI.png){}
:::

::: {.funder-card}
![](website-assets/images/wellcome-logo-black.png)
:::

::: {.funder-card}
![](website-assets/images/UOX-logo.jpg)
:::
:::
